idea: cuBLAS reference baseline
Baseline 7-kernel fused MoE pipeline from tutorial (00_baseline.co)
Fuse scatter into WGMMA, count+build, quant+sort+gather to reduce 7 kernels to 4
Apply all tutorial optimizations: parallel.async, grid swap, L2 persistence, QSG pipelining, Hillis-Steele scan, CUDA Graphs. DMA falls back to scalar copies (alignment issue).
Replace opt_copy (scalar 32-bit loads) with cp.async tiled_copy using SM80_CP_ASYNC_CACHEALWAYS<uint128_t> for LHS DMA. 128-bit coalesced loads via cp.async pipeline eliminate 60% L1TEX stalls.
Add __restrict__ to all kernel pointer params and --use_fast_math. +1.3% vs iter004.
Remove --maxrregcount=224 to allow more registers. Slightly worse, register cap helps occupancy.
Double-buffer K-loop pipelining (sA+sB x2). Regressed 7.2% due to doubled shared memory (49KB) reducing occupancy.
Added #pragma unroll to warp loops, fragment init, and scatter loops. No effect — compiler already unrolling at -O3.
Reduced maxrregcount from 224 to 128 for higher occupancy. Severe regression (-14%) from register spilling outweighing occupancy gains.
Changed cp.async from CACHEALWAYS to CACHEGLOBAL for LHS loads — bypass L1 for streaming data, frees L1 for scatter atomicAdd. +0.4% gain.
Changed L2 persistence from LHS to scatter output. Regressed 10% — LHS benefits more from L2 pinning due to cross-expert reuse.
Preloaded scale_b into registers + CACHEGLOBAL. Severe regression (-24%) from register spilling due to 16 extra floats per thread.
Added -Xptxas -dlcm=cg for all loads. No change — CACHEGLOBAL already explicit on LHS.

Double-buffer only RHS TMA sB 16KB keeping LHS single-buffered. Total shared 40KB. TMA prefetch next K-tile before WGMMA. Regressed 3.4% due to C7520 WGMMA serialization from divergent TMA barriers and increased shared memory reducing occupancy.
Added __launch_bounds__(128, 2) to all 3 WGMMA kernels. No measurable improvement - compiler already optimizing register usage well at 168 regs.

Moved warpgroup_commit_batch+wait inside K-loop before scale_accumulator. Eliminates compiler-injected C7517 warpgroup.wait/arrive. Same perf as iter010 - compiler was already handling this correctly.

STRUCTURAL: Replace cp.async LHS loads with TMA bulk tensor copy, freeing all 128 threads from memory load work. Only thread 0 issues TMA for both LHS and RHS via single barrier. Eliminates cp.async fence+wait pattern.
Reduce register pressure from 168 to 128 via maxrregcount=128 to increase occupancy from 18.75% to ~31%. Register spilling hurt more than occupancy gain helped.

STRUCTURAL: Double-buffer both LHS and RHS TMA loads with 2 barriers. Prologue+prefetch overlap. C7520 WGMMA serialization warning persisted due to conditional TMA issue. Doubled shared memory to 49KB. Regression from 12.88 to 12.07 TFLOPS.
Change global load cache policy to cache-global (-Xptxas -dlcm=cg) bypassing L1 for streaming loads. No measurable change vs iter017 baseline (12.88 vs 12.89).

STRUCTURAL: Overlap TMA prefetch with scale_accumulator. Prologue issues TMA for k=0, loop body: wait TMA, WGMMA, commit+wait, issue TMA for k+1, then scale_accumulator runs while TMA is in flight. Single-buffer approach avoids C7520.

Prefetch scale_a and scale_b into registers before TMA wait and WGMMA. Inline scale+accumulate loop replaces scale_accumulator function call, using prefetched values. Eliminates global memory loads from critical path.

Prefetch scatter metadata (sorted_route_ids, topk_weights) into registers and unroll scatter loops. No measurable improvement - scatter epilogue is not on critical path (runs once per M-tile vs 16x K-loop).

STRUCTURAL: Swap K/M loop order so RHS loaded once per K-tile, shared across M-tiles. Massive regression to 7.67 TFLOPS due to doubled barrier synchronization count (48 vs 32) and reduced TMA batch efficiency (separate 16KB+8KB vs combined 24KB).
Preload all 16 scale_a and scale_b values into registers before K-loop. Regression to 12.02T due to register pressure (48 extra registers causing spilling to local memory).
Add pragma unroll to K-loop, WGMMA inner loop, scatter column loop. No improvement - nvcc -O3 already unrolls these small constant-bound loops.
Move TMA issue before warpgroup_wait. INCORRECT: causes shared memory hazard - TMA overwrites sA/sB while WGMMA still reading. Verification failed.
Reduce maxrregcount from 224 to 160. Spilling offsets occupancy gain. No net improvement (12.99 vs 13.00 TFLOPS).
Changed L2 persistence from LHS to output buffer. Regression to 12.97T - LHS persistence is more valuable than output persistence.
